page: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 32 physical cores and 64 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across compute and memory-bound tasks. The instance achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2181.0 and a multi-core score of 20357.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 82965.26. Memory bandwidth is notable, with read-only small block cached operations reaching 5122.92 GB/sec and large block uncached operations at 192.31 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which contribute to an LLM prompt processing speed of 3744.69 tokens/sec for a small 135M model and 290.6 tokens/sec for a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8i-flex.16xlarge provides a dense memory-to-core ratio and high-speed DDR5 RAM, making it a resource-dense option for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of local storage. The lack of bundled local NVMe storage represents a tradeoff, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data. This instance is suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 512.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory, delivering 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but provides a baseline network speed of 15 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show strong capabilities, with a Passmark CPU score of 82965.26 and a Redis SET throughput of 113.45 million operations per second. The processor supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX, enabling efficient vector and matrix calculations. This hardware profile is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, large-scale data processing, software compilation, and LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, designed for in-memory databases and data-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring 512 GB of DDR5 RAM and Intel Xeon processors for high-throughput database and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores via Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor at 3.9 GHz
512.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory (8.0 GB per core)
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Optimized for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and software compilation
